title: 'Will talk in 2 days': BJP leader Annamalai on question of starting new party
content: Former Tamil Nadu BJP president K Annamalai has fueled speculation about his political future by traveling to Delhi and offering a cryptic "Please wait" when asked about reports of launching a new party. This comes amid posters from supporters in Coimbatore calling for him to lead, amplifying discussions about his next move after the 2026 assembly elections.

title: Sooryavanshi rues error after RR's Qualifier 2 exit: 'Maine ek mistake kari'
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi took responsibility for Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 Qualifier 2 defeat, admitting they were 10-12 runs short against Gujarat Titans. The 15-year-old felt he should have kept more of the strike on a difficult pitch instead of exposing new batters. Despite the heartbreak, Sooryavanshi enjoyed a sensational season, finishing as IPL 2026's highest run-scorer with 776 runs.

title: '30 lakh fake accounts': West Bengal govt orders SIT probe into Lakshmir Bhandar scheme
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced a special investigation team and money laundering probe into alleged fake Lakshmir Bhandar accounts. He revealed approximately 30 lakh accounts were fraudulent, with men also receiving benefits under the previous TMC government.  The Chief Minister emphasized this action to prevent illegalities in future welfare schemes.

title: Google plans to release 32 million Wolbachia-infected mosquitoes across Florida and California
content: Alphabet's Debug initiative is seeking EPA approval to release 32 million specially treated male mosquitoes in California and Florida. This innovative approach uses Wolbachia bacteria to sterilize wild Culex mosquitoes, aiming to significantly reduce the spread of diseases like West Nile virus. Advanced AI and robotics are enabling this large-scale effort, with trials planned over two years.

title: 'Amazon would collapse if run like New York City': Jeff Bezos takes swipe at Mamdani
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has endorsed New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's new government efficiency initiative, COGE. This comes after their public disagreement on taxing the wealthy. Bezos, who previously criticized city spending, now supports Mamdani's plan to make government work smarter, suggesting savings could benefit lower earners.

title: ICC suspends Cricket Canada after corruption allegations: What it means for players
content: "The ICC Board resolved to suspend Cricket Canada from ICC membership with immediate effect due to serious breaches of its membership obligations," the ICC said in a statement.

title: MiraOne founder explains why one-size-fits-all healthcare is fading
content: MiraOne offers a comprehensive blood test combining genetic insights and medicine-response analysis for personalized wellness. Founded by Nickhil Jakatdar and Abhinav Kejriwal, it translates complex data into actionable recommendations, addressing the healthcare challenge of truly personalized medicine. This innovative approach empowers individuals to make informed health decisions and enhances the doctor-patient relationship

title: Google AI CEO has a message for laid off engineers at Meta, Amazon, Block, and other companies
content: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis argues companies cutting engineers due to AI are misguided. He believes increased productivity should fuel more projects, not layoffs. Hassabis sees AI as an opportunity to expand ambitions, not shrink workforces, and is eager to hire talent shed by rivals for new ventures like drug discovery and game design.

title: After Assam, MP next? CM Mohan Yadav says state committed to implementing UCC
content: Madhya Pradesh is moving towards a Uniform Civil Code, with Chief Minister Mohan Yadav announcing a committee to gather public feedback from all communities. Inspired by other BJP-ruled states, the government aims to establish a common legal framework for marriage, family, and succession matters, urging citizens to share their views via a dedicated website.

title: Upcoming car launches in June 2026: New S-Class, BMW X6 & more
content: The Sealion 6 is also under consideration and has been seen on Indian roads during testing. Globally, it is offered with either a naturally aspirated or turbocharged 1.5-litre petrol engine, paired with 18.3kWh and 26.6kWh battery packs. BYD claims a maximum combined driving range of up to 1,092km.

title: Official: Zee Entertainment's Unite8 Sports to broadcast FIFA World Cup in India
content: Football fans in India can rejoice as Zee Entertainment Enterprises Limited (ZEEL) has secured broadcast rights for the 2026 and 2030 FIFA World Cups, along with the 2027 FIFA Women's World Cup and other FIFA events until 2034.

title: How Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s father’s faith shaped his extraordinary success
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi made IPL history, securing five individual awards in one season. His father's unwavering dedication, including selling land to fund his cricket dream, fueled this success.  A former cricketer himself, his father instilled the crucial lesson that team victory trumps personal milestones, shaping Vaibhav's remarkable journey.

title: OC, CC, RERA and sale deed: Property documents every homebuyer should know
content: Buying a home involves crucial paperwork. Key documents like the Occupancy Certificate (OC), Completion Certificate (CC), RERA registration, and Sale Deed are vital.  Understanding these ensures legal compliance, protects your investment, and prevents future disputes.  Verify these carefully before finalizing your property purchase for peace of mind.

title: Israel captures Beaufort Castle in Lebanon: What makes the 900-year-old fortress strategic target
content: In a daring military maneuver, Israeli forces have taken control of the iconic Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon, marking a pivotal moment in the ongoing conflict. This strategic victory, achieved amid fierce clashes, boosts Israeli oversight over crucial territories. As this unfolds, U.S.

title: Social media influencer Manasi found dead in Kanpur; family claims dowry harassment
content: Social media influencer Mansi was found dead in Kanpur under suspicious circumstances. Her family alleges dowry-related harassment and murder by her husband and in-laws, claiming the incident was staged as a suicide. Protests have erupted, demanding justice and strict action against the accused.

title: From Agni 5 to Akash & hypersonics: Decoding India's homegrown arsenal & defence shield
content: India is rapidly advancing its indigenous defence capabilities, moving from import dependence to becoming a global player.  From long-range missiles and nuclear submarines to anti-drone systems and hypersonic technology, the nation is building a resilient arsenal.  This strategic shift enhances national security and positions India as a key partner in defence innovation and production.

title: 'Why hate India?' BJP hits back at Kapil Sibal over 'ashamed to live in India' remark
content: BJP has strongly criticized Congress MP Kapil Sibal for his remarks on democracy, accusing him of "hating India" and selectively ignoring violence. Sibal's comments followed an alleged attack on TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee in West Bengal. The BJP questioned Sibal's silence on past incidents while criticizing the ruling party.

title: Eagles, Cowboys linked to Browns' $60M star in trade rumors
content: Trade speculation around Myles Garrett has resurfaced after June 1, with the Cleveland Browns facing a key decision on their $160 million defensive superstar. While no trade is imminent, teams such as the Philadelphia Eagles, Dallas Cowboys, Chicago Bears, and Carolina Panthers have been mentioned as potential landing spots. Any deal would likely require a massive return, as Garrett remains one of the NFL's most dominant pass rushers.

title: UPI clocked its biggest month in May, transactions surpass Rs 29.90 lakh crore
content: India's UPI transactions reached record highs in May, with 23.2 billion transactions valued at Rs 29.90 lakh crore. This growth, driven by summer travel and seasonal spending, signifies a healthy upward trajectory for the digital payments ecosystem. Future expansion is anticipated through Credit-on-UPI and cross-border initiatives.

title: Buying a home? Check if you qualify for PMAY Urban 2.0 benefits
content: The Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Urban 2.0 offers affordable housing solutions for low and middle-income families in cities. Through initiatives like Beneficiary Led Construction and Affordable Rental Housing, the scheme aims to provide financial assistance and accessible living spaces. Eligibility is based on income and requires no prior pucca house ownership.

title: 'All major issues settled': Centre's big update on US-India trade deal
content: India and the United States are nearing the finalization of their first bilateral trade agreement tranche, with most key details settled. A high-level US delegation is in New Delhi for crucial negotiations, aiming to conclude an interim pact. Both nations are confident of signing the agreement soon, building on a framework established in February.

title: Explained: The reasons behind India’s falling infant mortality rate
content: India has seen a significant drop in infant mortality, with the rate falling to 24 deaths per 1,000 live births in 2024, largely due to increased institutional deliveries. However, stark disparities persist, with states like Kerala and Goa nearing developed-world standards while others like Chhattisgarh struggle despite improved healthcare access.

title: India, Australia strengthen defence ties as China asserts dominance in Indo-Pacific
content: India and Australia have significantly boosted their defence ties, agreeing to enhance interoperability, expand maritime security cooperation, and deepen military engagements. Key initiatives include joint exercises, reciprocal aircraft deployments, and increased information sharing. Discussions also focused on defence industrial collaboration, with plans for joint research and industry roundtables, strengthening their Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.

title: TMC expels 2 MLAs, Sandipan Saha and Ritabrata Banerjee, for 'anti-party activities'
content: The Trinamool Congress has expelled two MLAs, Sandipan Saha and Ritabrata Banerjee, from its primary membership due to alleged anti-party activities and actions detrimental to the organisation. Both legislators reportedly failed to attend party meetings and made statements against the AITC's interests.

title: Annamalai posters fuel speculation over new political outfit in Tamil Nadu
content: Giant posters in Coimbatore suggest K Annamalai may launch a new political movement. Supporters are calling for his leadership. This follows speculation about differences with the BJP. Annamalai's political future is a topic of discussion. He has been a prominent BJP face in Tamil Nadu. His next move remains unclear.

title: 'Int'l terrorism': Kremlin fumes after France seizes Russian-linked tanker
content: France has detained a Russian-linked oil tanker, the Tagor, in the Atlantic, suspecting it of violating sanctions imposed over the Ukraine war. Russia vehemently condemned the seizure as illegal, bordering on 'international terrorism,' and vowed to protect its shipping. This action is part of a wider Western effort to curb Russia's 'shadow fleet' used to circumvent sanctions.

title: Kylian Mbappé-Ester Expósito romance sparks outrage amid Bad Bunny buzz
content: Real Madrid star player Kylian Mbappé is keeping his eyes on the World Cup as he gears up for it even as his relationship with girlfriend, popular actress Ester Expósito after her controversial moment with Bad Bunny, sparking outrage among millions. While Kylian Mbappé has remained distant from the drama, Ester Expósito and Bad Bunny have sparked outrage for “disrespecting” the star player.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Packers, Lions linked to Colts QB in $33M move
content: The Indianapolis Colts’ star player Anthony Richardson is actively seeking a trade since the last few weeks even as he continues to attend the team’s OTAs, sparking confusion among all the fans. While a number of teams have been linked to him, it seems the Green Bay Packers and the Detroit Lions have emerged as strong landing spots for the star player.

title: Diljit Dosanjh on headlining London’s Wembley Stadium
content: Punjabi superstar Diljit Dosanjh will perform at London's Wembley Stadium on September 12, 2026 — a first for South Asian artists. Announcing it at his Toronto show, Diljit called it historic, emotionally recalling his mother's belief in him, though she'd never heard of Wembley.

title: Twisha Sharma death : CBI uses dummies to recreate crime scene, questions Giribala Singh
content: The CBI meticulously recreated the crime scene at former judge Giribala Singh's residence, employing dummies and sacks to reconstruct the events surrounding Twisha Sharma's death. This crucial step aims to pinpoint discrepancies and clarify the incident's circumstances. Singh, now in CBI custody after her anticipatory bail was revoked, is undergoing intensive questioning as the investigation progresses.

title: India-Oman trade pact: Govt's 'Plan B' amid Hormuz crisis
content: Amidst Middle East chaos impacting the Strait of Hormuz, India's new free trade agreement with Oman, effective June 1, offers a strategic alternative. Oman's location outside the Strait provides a reliable gateway for trade and energy, as evidenced by recent import surges from the nation.

title: RCB win celebrations turn chaotic: Videos show fans burning crackers, climbing vehicles
content: Royal Challengers Bangalore fans' jubilant street celebrations following their IPL win led to disruptions and a police lathi-charge to disperse the crowd. Videos showed firecrackers, property damage, and traffic jams near the Secretariat. Despite the chaos and criticism, no legal action has been taken yet, raising questions about the spirit of sportsmanship.

title: Inside RCB's winning machine: The masterminds behind back-to-back IPL titles
content: RCB's back-to-back IPL titles were built on more than performances on the field. From Andy Flower and Mo Bobat to Dinesh Karthik, Malolan Rangarajan and Omkar Salvi, a formidable backroom team laid the foundations for the franchise's sustained success.

title: Why Nepal PM's 'encroaching' India remark has sparked row: Border dispute explained
content: In a recent address, Nepal's Prime Minister ignited tensions by addressing Indian 'encroachments' along the border, which necessitated a swift clarification from officials in Kathmandu. The territorial dispute involving Kalapani, Lipulekh, and Limpiyadhura stretches back to a treaty signed in 1816 and hinges on the interpretation of the Kali River's source.

title: 'We thank India': Africa CDC on emergency Ebola aid as Congo battles Ebola outbreak
content: India has dispatched vital medical supplies, including diagnostics and therapeutics, to aid the Democratic Republic of Congo's fight against the escalating Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak. The Africa CDC expressed deep gratitude for this crucial assistance, which was channeled through its regional hub in Uganda and is now being deployed to affected areas.

title: 'I think you can find the humour in a situation without...': Quote of the day by Steve Carell
content: Steve Carell, a beloved actor, champions a rare comedic philosophy: finding humor without cruelty. His career, from 'The Office' to 'The Big Short,' showcases a fundamental decency, prioritizing sincerity and dignity over mockery. Carell believes in laughing with characters, not at them, a principle that defines his impactful performances and resonates deeply with audiences.

title: Gujarat's Halpati Housing Scheme explained: Check eligibility and how to apply
content: Gujarat's Halpati Housing Scheme offers Rs 1.2 lakh aid to Talaviya and Nayak families in southern districts for home construction. Beneficiaries rural areas of southern Gujarat can apply. No income limit applies, and funds are disbursed in phases directly to bank accounts. Essential documents include age and caste proof, birth certificate, and ration card.

title: Brahmotsavam at TTD’s Karvetinagaram temple from June 6
content: TTD EO directs officials to make elaborate arrangements for devotees attending the annual festivities

title: Collector promotes fuel conservation by riding electric bike in Puttaparthi
content: Launching the second phase of an awareness drive, Shyam Prasad appeals to the public to reduce fuel consumption and adopt environmentally sustainable alternatives

title: NEET paper leak: Delhi court sends three accused to 14 days’ judicial custody
content: Special Judge Ajay Gupta allowed the CBI’s plea seeking judicial custody of physics translator Manisha Sanjay Hawaldar, paediatrician Dr. Manoj Shirure and physics teacher Tejas Harshadkumar Shah

title: Myanmar assures India its territory won’t be used against New Delhi’s security interests
content: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri said the talks between the two sides focused on the entire gamut of bilateral issues that included trade and economic ties, defence and security related issues, border management, development assistance as well as the regional situation.

title: Palaniswami appeals to his party members not to  carry out ‘undesirable and depressing’ acts
content: He stated this against the backdrop of the death of joint secretary of traders’ wing in the party’s Thanjavur (East) district unit K.S. Mahendran

title: BJP attacks Kapil Sibal over 'ashamed to live in India' remark
content: Attack came in response to Mr. Sibal's remarks on May 31 that he was ashamed to live in a country where the party in power would use any means to destroy the foundations of democracy, referring to the attack on TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee

title: From Andal to the Raman Effect, new book captures Srivilliputhur’s many legacies
content: The 168-page publication of The Hindu titled Srivilliputhur - Divine Realm of Andal, curated by Suganthy Krishnamachari, traces the religious, cultural, and historical significance of Srivilliputhur close to Madurai

title: Hijab ban rollback: Sri Rama Sene distributes saffron shawls to college students in Hubballi
content: As a mark of protest to Government’s notification allowing hijab in educational institutions, members ofSri Rama Sene distributed saffron shawls to colleges students in Hubballi on Monday.

title: RJD leaders slam NDA govt. for asking Rabri Devi to vacate bungalow, term it ‘vendetta politics’
content: Rashtriya Janata Dal principal national general secretary Abdul Bari Siddiqui accused the government of ‘targeting the opposition’ for criticising its policies

title: EC reviews progress of SIR in Sikkim, asks BLOs to assist voters in filling up forms
content: The enumeration phase of the exercise commenced on Saturday (May 30, 2026) and will continue till June 28

title: Dhinakaran appeals to TVK government to shut down unauthorised bars
content: He made this appeal in light of a teenage girl’s killing in Chennai on Saturday night, after an SUV rammed a two-wheeler she was riding pillion on

title: India, Australia underscore maritime freedom and supply chain security in high-level talks
content: Ministers Rajnath Singh and Richard Marles announced that the two countries would begin developing an MoU regarding the provision of defence articles and defence services as the next step

title: Delhi HC recognises ‘right to be forgotten’, directs search engines to disable ‘name-based’ search in court orders, news
content: Delhi HC ordered authorities, search engine operators and legal database platforms to de-index and disable their "name-based search functionality" in respect of judgments, orders and news articles cited by the petitioners

title: Supreme Court declines plea to hold NEET re-test in computer-based mode
content: Top court expresses disinclination to change mode of June 21 re-test and defers consideration of matter until July, after conclusion of court’s partial working days

title: Why football’s most passionate fans may be in India
content: In Kerala, West Bengal and several other places across India, the FIFA World Cup is a homecoming — of fan clubs, midnight screenings, bike rallies and WhatsApp groups that spring back to life every four years

title: Punjab’s Hoshiarpur learns differently as VHMI spearheads Montessori initiative in govt. schools
content: As a part of Punjab’s first Montessori education initiative in government Anganwadis and pre-primary schools launched in collaboration with Chandigarh-based Vivek High Montessori Institute (VHMI), the project is reshaping the early learning landscape

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: NH-544G: ₹5,378-crore Bengaluru-Vijayawada corridor to cut 110 km and up to 4 hours off the journey
content: The corridor links NH-44 and NH-16, runs through five districts and opens direct access to the Hindupuram and Kopparthi industrial nodes

title: Kerala aims to raise daily milk production to 1 crore litres in three years: V.D. Satheesan
content: The UDF government will adopt measures to bridge the gap between production and demand, the Chief Minister said

title: Fire breaks out in SPA building in central Delhi, no casualties
content: Firefighters launched an operation to contain the flames that had erupted in faculty rooms of the administrative block situated on the second floor of the SPA building

title: Poor run of Telangana finances continues in the new fiscal with just 7.43% of target achieved
content: State borrowed ₹11,413 crore during the month to meet immediate requirements, according to provisional figures submitted to the Comptroller and Auditor General of India

title: Will not allow senior advocates to argue during partial court working days: SC
content: The apex court said it was being done to encourage young lawyers to argue their cases during the partial court working days from today till July 12

title: Release 20 civilians held hostage, two northeast CMs appeal to Manipur captors
content: Nagaland Chief Minister Neiphiu Rio seeks Home Minister Amit Shah’s intervention for the release of six Liangmai Nagas

title: Nedumangad child murder case: Police expand probe into couple’s pasts
content: In the aftermath of the toddler’s death, fresh allegations and previously reported incidents linked to Ashkar and Akhila’s personal lives have come under renewed scrutiny

title: Cockroach Janta Party founder to return to India on June 6
content: In a video released on Instagram, Abhijeet Dipke called on supporters and students to join a peaceful protest in Delhi seeking the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: Who was Sathyendra? The Chennai cinephile who always had questions
content: The cinephile, whose viral Leo review made headlines, passed away recently. Chennai’s film community rose up to the occasion

title: For every ₹100 spent on healthcare in Telangana, households paid ₹39 from their own pockets: NHA report
content: The findings suggest that while the government remained the largest contributor to healthcare spending in Telangana, households continued to bear a substantial share of medical costs through direct payments at the point of care

title: SPARSH service centre for defence pensioners inaugurated in Guntur
content: A SPARSH service centre of the Defence Accounts Department opens in Guntur to offer free pension services to Army, Navy and Air Force retirees

title: TDP leader accuses YSRCP of defaming Naidu government over DSC recruitment
content: TDP leader Boina Govinda Rajulu accuses the YSR Congress Party of trying to defame the Naidu government over the DSC recruitment drive

title: India, Oman free trade pact comes into force
content: Bilateral trade between India and Oman reached $11.18 billion in FY 2025-26, up from $10.61 billion in FY 2024-25

title: SIT to probe fake accounts of Lakshmir Bhandar scheme of TMC government: CM Suvendu Adhikari
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said around 30 lakh Lakshmir Bhandar accounts are fake and has asked the director general of police to form an Special Investigation Team to investigate the matter

title: Minister Janardhan Reddy launches plantation drive in Nandyal district
content: Planting a sapling is not enough. People must also take full responsibility for their protection and upkeep, the Minister said

title: Arsenal fans turn Kochi screening into a night to remember
content: Over 1,000 Arsenal fans from across Kerala and abroad gathered for one of India’s largest football fan-club screenings

title: Fire at Mobis plant near Chennai disrupts Hyundai car production
content: “The company and Mobis teams are working closely to assess the situation and extent of damage to the Mobis facility. This incident will result in temporary disruption to company’s production,” Hyundai said

title: AIADMK functionary found dead in Thanjavur; Palaniswami announces ₹55-lakh solatium
content: The police discovered a video posted by the functionary on social media, stating he was upset over the recent developments in the AIADMK, and had thus decided to take the extreme step of ending his life

title: PM SVANidhi transformed lives of countless street vendors, says PM Modi
content: The PM SVANidhi was launched during the COVID-19 pandemic to provide affordable working capital loans to street vendors whose businesses had been severely affected

title: Farmers stage protest seeking implementation of crop loan waiver promise in Erode
content: The protesters accused the TVK-led of neglecting farmers’ welfare despite assurances given during the Assembly election campaign

title: PM Modi holds talks with Myanmar President Aung Hlaing
content: PM Narendra Modi held wide-ranging talks with Myanmar President Min Aung Hlaing, focusing on trade, connectivity, border security and defence cooperation

title: Ahead of budget, Kerala CM V.D. Satheesan seeks support of banking sector for flagship programmes
content: Satheesan addressed the State Level Bankers’ Committee, his first after taking over as Chief Minister

title: Relieve PG teachers from Census duty to safeguard Class 12 students’ interests: association
content: The association said postgraduate teachers would be fully engaged in teaching and examination-related activities from June to December, a crucial period for students preparing for the higher secondary public examinations

title: West Bengal cabinet 2026: Full list of ministers in Suvendu Adhikari govt
content: BJP’s new government in West Bengal inducted several fresh faces into the Cabinet. Check the list of Ministers here

title: TMC expels two MLAs for 'anti-party' activities
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari told a press conference that the two had filed complaints in connection with the ‘fake signature’ case in the State Assembly

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Daughters killed for dowry
content: In the past three weeks, 33-year-old Twisha Sharma’s death over alleged dowry harassment has seen alleged procedural lapses and institutional bias by  Bhopal Police. As her mother-in-law, a retired judge, slandered her daughter-in-law, a former model on TV channels, Twisha’s parents and sibling stood up against the influential in-laws. An exhausting timeline of the case and the debate it has sparked about walking out of a bad marriage

title: Madras High Court closes DMK’s 2016 case related to conduct of local body polls in Tamil Nadu
content: Justice M. Dhandapani closes the case after taking note that Justice N. Kirubarakan (since retired) had already passed elaborate orders in the matter

title: Twisha Sharma death case: CBI recreates crime scene using dummies
content: CBI’s investigation intensifies as alleged dowry harassment case draws public outcry and demands for justice following the tragic death of 33-year-old Twisha Sharma

title: TDP leaders level land and forgery allegations against YSRCP’s Brahmanaidu
content: Senior TDP leaders level allegations against YSRCP leader Bolla Brahmanaidu over land transactions and the alleged use of forged documents

title: Kochi Corporation to install weighing machines to verify waste disposal estimates
content: Kochi Corporation is spending crores without any mechanism to verify quantity of waste provided by contracted companies

title: CM Revanth Reddy for crop diversification, as below normal rainfall is forecast
content: Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy has directed officials to educate farmers on the impacts of El Niño and the usage of less water-intensive crops, as below normal rainfall is forecast for monsoon

title: Kerala to get academic centres of excellence, says CM Satheesan
content: V.D. Satheesan was speaking after inaugurating the State-level school Pravesanotsavam in the State capital on June 1, 2026

title: 75 vehicles seized in Bengaluru during 2026 IPL final night crackdown
content: A special enforcement drive was conducted as part of enhanced security arrangements for the high-profile cricket match

title: K Annamalai Likely To Quit BJP, Launch Own Party: Sources
content: K Annamalai, IPS officer-turned-politician, was offered a Rajya Sabha seat, sources have told NDTV. The 41-year-old leader, NDTV has learnt, refused the offer.

title: 'I See Everything In Black And White': Vijay On Questions On His Suit
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay arrived at Tiruchirappalli - the same place from where he launched his campaign in September 2025 - this evening and went on a roadshow

title: Man's Severed Legs Found In Odisha Forest, Tiger Attack Suspected
content: Severed legs of a man were found inside Similipal Tiger Reserve in Odisha's Mayurbhanj district on Monday, with locals alleging that he was killed in a tiger attack, officials said.

title: The Night Paris Plunged Into Chaos: Riots, Death, Mayhem Grip City Streets
content: Paris Saint-Germain retained the Champions League title on Saturday, beating Arsenal 4-3 on penalties in Budapest, but the joy of victory gave way to destruction on the streets of France's capital.

title: "Friendly Call Is Often Bait": J&K Drive To Save Men From Pak Spy Network
content: The initiative aims to prevent young people from falling prey to ISI honeytraps or unknowingly sharing sensitive information such as troop movement.

title: "Consent Is Key": Supreme Court On Voluntary Sex Work vs Trafficking
content: The Supreme Court said that "consent" remains the central legal differentiator in determining whether a case involves trafficking or voluntary adult sex work.

title: US, Iran Keep Launching Attacks On Each Other As Negotiations Stall
content: The latest exchange of fire coincided with Israel expanding its offensive into Lebanon, with Prime Minister Netanyahu vowing to push deeper into the country.

title: Probe Shows Forced Conversion Bid In TCS Nashik Case: Devendra Fadnavis
content: Devendra Fadnavis said that when religion is used as a motive to target women, it points to 'love jihad', and that there is a law to counter this.

title: Bengal Cabinet Expansion: Meet 35 New Faces In Suvendu Adhikari's Government
content: The expansion includes 13 Cabinet Ministers, three Ministers of State (Independent Charge) and 19 Ministers of State.

title: Supreme Court Gets 5 New Judges, Total Strength Rises To 37
content: Acting on the recommendation of the Supreme Court Collegium, the Centre on Monday cleared the elevation of four High Court Chief Justices and senior advocate V. Mohana as judges of the Supreme Court.

title: Signature Scandal Explodes In Bengal, Trinamool Expels 2 MLAs
content: Suvendu Adhikari today named two Trinamool MLAs who had complained over the forgery of their signatures in a letter endorsing Sobhandeb Chattopadhyay.

title: Delhi's Jan-May Average AQI Lowest In 8 Years Barring 2020
content: Delhi recorded its cleanest air for the January-May period in eight years, excluding the Covid-affected year of 2020, with the average Air Quality Index (AQI) improving to 211 this year, according to data shared by the Commission for Air Quality Management (CAQM) on Monday.

title: Man, 26, Rapes 80-Year-Old Woman in UP's Hathras, Arrested: Cops
content: An elderly woman was allegedly raped by her 28-year-old neighbour at her residence in Uttar Pradesh's Hathras on Monday, police said.

title: 5-Day Relief For Kerala Firm, Probe Against P Vijayan's Daughter To Continue
content: The company has been under the probe agency's scanner over allegations that it made illegal payments to Exalogic Solutions, an IT company owned by Veena Thaikkandiyil.

title: After 'May-hem' On D-Street, Why June Could Be A Turning Point For Markets
content: Sensex, Nifty News: June is unlikely to be a straight-line recovery. The same factors that hurt markets in May have not disappeared.

title: Cops Raid Akali Dal's Bikram Majithia's Home Over Police Station 'Attack' Case
content: According to the police, Bikram Majithia was part of an unlawful gathering of people, which attempted to free the detainee Jobanpreet at Majitha police station on Sunday.

title: Telangana CID Busts Interstate Illegal Dafabet Betting Racket, 11 Arrested
content: CID's Special Investigation Team (SIT) traced the victim's funds through a complex web of 46 mule bank accounts operating across eight layers, exposing a sophisticated network spanning multiple states.

title: CBSE OSM row: Education ministry begins crackdown over alleged irregularities
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: Is Israel derailing US-Iran peace talks? Lebanon strikes cast shadow over negotiations
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers.
